column2: Brent crude surged to $112.57/bbl on Friday March 27 (WTI $99.64) as Trump's April 6 ultimatum to Iran drove renewed escalation fears — reversing the earlier de-escalation selloff. VIX rose to 31.05. Global gold prices set at $4,493/oz (Kitco). TTF eased to 55.22 EUR/MWh. US gasoline: $3.981/gal (AAA). Vietnam fuel prices cut on March 26 as advance funding for the fuel price stabilization fund were secured: RON95 fell to 24,332 VND/L from its 30,690 peak; diesel to 35,440 VND/L. VN-Index recovered 1.71% to 1,672.80 on March 27 after heavy losses. Vietcombank USD/VND: 26,230 (Mar 27); unofficial market rate 28,024 (Mar 29). Hormuz remains effectively closed on Day 29. Iran's April 6 deadline is the next key inflection point.
US launches airstrikes on Iranian nuclear facilities; Iran threatens Strait closure
IRGC Navy deploys mines and fast boats across Hormuz shipping lanes
Ship transits drop to zero; Brent surges past $86/bbl
All major carriers announce Hormuz transit suspension
Vietnam MOIT emergency fuel price adjustment; RON95 to 22340 VND/L. P&I clubs withdraw war-risk coverage.
Vietnam Govt issues Resolution 36 on petroleum price stabilization
EIA confirms Brent at $94/bbl; Israel strikes Iran South Pars gas field
PM Pham Minh Chinh directs fuel price stabilization; Price Stabilization Fund activated. RON95 briefly hits 29120.
Extraordinary adjustment: RON95 pulled back to 25240 (heavy PSF subsidy)
SBV intervenes; VN-Index falls below 1700. Unofficial market USD/VND hits 27096
RON95 surges 20% to 30690 VND/L; diesel +24% to 33420; PM calls partners for oil supply
Brent hits $113.40. Vietnam secures 4M barrels from partners. VN-Index drops to 1648.
Day 22 of crisis. Goldman warns oil may stay triple-digits for years.
Unofficial market USD/VND hits 27307. SBV reference rate unchanged at 25085. Iran says it will target US/Israeli assets if energy facilities struck.

VN-Index falls 2.08% to 1614. SBV ref rate +5 VND to 25090. Vietcombank sell hits band ceiling 26344. Chợ đen USD/VND surges to 27950-28000. Gold near $4373 (-10% weekly). Oil steady as markets weigh Iran-US rhetoric.
Brent falls to $98.83 and WTI to $90.67 continuing the retreat from Trump's Iran strike postponement. S&P 500 +1.4%. VIX eases to 24.85. Gold slides further to $4

column2: Trump's April 6 deadline to Iran to reopen Hormuz confirmed. Brent settles at $112.57
VN-Index +1.71% to 1672.80. Brent settle $112.57. Trump confirms April 6 Iran deadline.
Iran attacks Kuwaiti tanker near Dubai. WTI settles $102.88 above $100 first time since July 2022. Brent $115.35. VN-Index -1.35% to 1650. VIX 30.61.
US gasoline national avg crosses $4/gal ($4.018 per AAA) for first time since 2022. Gold $4567.
